deep_dive.title: WCog-VLA: A Dual-Level World-Cognitive Vision-Language-Action Model for End-to-End Autonomous Driving
deep_dive.abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have advanced end-to-end autonomous driving. However, existing methods either lack comprehensive world cognition or suffer from fragmented world foresight, inherently confining these models to reactive driving. To address this limitation, we propose WCog-VLA, a novel dual-level World-Cognitive VLA framework that successfully bridges semantic world forecasting with generative world evolution to achieve proactive autonomous driving. At the semantic level, WCog-VLA unifies world cognition and reasoning by incorporating 3D spatial perception and injecting agent tokens to capture the world dynamics, while concurrently enabling Game-theoretic Chain-of-Thought (Game-CoT) reasoning. At the generative level, we introduce the Aligned Decoupled Diffusion Transformer (ADDT) as a powerful generative world model that synthesizes physically-plausible joint multi-agent trajectories. Through scene representation alignment, ADDT reduces the number of denoising steps required and thus significantly accelerates inference. To facilitate strategic reasoning, we further construct a large-scale dataset featuring 85k Game-CoT annotations. Extensive experiments on the NAVSIM benchmark demonstrate that WCog-VLA achieves a State-Of-The-Art (SOTA) PDMS score of 92.9.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: world-cognitive VLA model for planning and prediction in autonomous driving
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于自动驾驶规划与预测的世界认知VLA模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于自动驾驶规划与预测的世界认知VLA模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于自动驾驶规划与预测的世界认知VLA模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes WCog-VLA, a dual-level world-cognitive framework bridging semantic world forecasting and generative world evolution for proactive autonomous driving, using 3D spatial perception, agent tokens, and game-theoretic CoT reasoning.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有端到端自动驾驶模型缺乏全面世界认知或碎片化世界预见，导致被动驾驶。为此提出WCog-VLA双层级世界认知视觉-语言-动作框架，在语义层融合3D空间感知与智能体标记捕捉世界动态，并启用博弈论思维链推理，在生成层进行世界演化，实现主动规划与预测。实验表明其在复杂驾驶场景中显著优于基线。

deep_dive.title: ComplexMimic: Human-Scene Interaction Imitation in Complex 3D Environments
deep_dive.abstract: Physics-based Human-Scene Interaction (HSI) imitation learning is crucial for embodied intelligence as it bridges the gap between kinematic 3D motions and real-world dynamics. However, most existing methods focus on simplified scene settings, leaving complex environments largely unexplored, which limits their applicability in real-world scenarios. In this paper, we focus on HSI mimicry in complex environments. Under this complex setting, we observe an inherent trade-off between successfully performing interaction and maintaining natural, physically plausible motions. To address this challenge, we propose ComplexMimic, a framework that reconstructs diverse HSI by interpreting imperfect MoCap data. First, we introduce a Dual Flow Strategy, which learns two complementary experts: an imitation expert for accurate motion tracking and an interaction expert for collision-aware adaptation in complex scenes. Second, naive multi-expert distillation, which treats all experts equally, often under-samples challenging behaviors, limiting effective learning. To mitigate this issue, we propose a difficulty-aware distillation strategy that adaptively weights supervision and prioritizes hard-yet-learnable trajectories guided by failure statistics and learning progress signals. Extensive experiments on three benchmark datasets demonstrate that our approach outperforms current state-of-the-art methods.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: physics-based HSI imitation learning from expert demonstrations
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于物理的人-场景交互模仿学习，从专家演示中学习策略
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于物理的人-场景交互模仿学习，从专家演示中学习策略
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于物理的人-场景交互模仿学习，从专家演示中学习策略
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: ComplexMimic addresses human-scene interaction imitation in complex 3D environments via a Dual Flow Strategy that learns natural motions from imperfect MoCap data, enabling embodied agents to mimic diverse interactions.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对复杂3D环境中人-场景交互模仿的挑战，ComplexMimic提出双流策略，从有缺陷的运动捕捉数据中学习物理合理且自然的交互动作，解决了交互成功与运动自然性之间的权衡问题。实验表明该方法在复杂场景下生成多样化的交互行为，为具身智能体在真实环境中模仿人类交互提供了有效框架。

deep_dive.title: Learning 4D Geometric Priors for Inference-Efficient World Action Models
deep_dive.abstract: World Action Models (WAMs) have shown strong potential for robotic manipulation by jointly modeling visual future dynamics and executable action sequences. However, existing video-action co-training methods primarily optimize appearance-oriented video latents, which may insufficiently capture the temporally evolving geometry required for precise manipulation. We propose MECo-WAM, a Multi-Expert Co-Training World Action Model that injects action-relevant 4D geometric priors into video-action representations while preserving the original lightweight inference graph. During training, MECo-WAM combines video and action experts with a lightweight 4D expert supervised by relational targets from a frozen VGGT encoder. Asymmetric expert visibility prevents non-causal shortcuts from auxiliary geometry to action generation. To transfer geometric knowledge into the deployed video-action pathway, we introduce decayed 4D read-mask attention, which provides restricted current-frame geometric guidance early in training and progressively removes this dependency. We further propose action-aware temporal geometric distillation, which aligns within-frame geometric relations and their temporal evolution while emphasizing visual regions most relevant to robot actions. At deployment, all auxiliary 4D components are removed. Experiments on LIBERO (98.2%), RoboTwin 2.0 (92.6%), and challenging real-world manipulation tasks show that MECo-WAM improves manipulation performance without increasing inference cost.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: World Action Models for robotic manipulation with 4D geometric priors
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于机器人操作的世界动作模型，注入4D几何先验
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于机器人操作的世界动作模型，注入4D几何先验
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于机器人操作的世界动作模型，注入4D几何先验
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: MECo-WAM injects 4D geometric priors into World Action Models for robotic manipulation, improving action-relevant representation without increasing inference cost.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有世界动作模型在视频-动作联合训练中主要优化外观，忽略时变几何信息。本文提出MECo-WAM，通过多专家联合训练注入4D几何先验，在保持轻量推理图的同时提升操控精度。该方法利用冻结的VGGT编码器提供关系目标，实验证明在精细操作任务中显著优于基线。为具身智能中的世界模型应用提供了新思路。

deep_dive.title: PriGo: Test-Time Primitive Guidance to Diffusion and Flow Policies for Adaptive Robotic Manipulation
deep_dive.abstract: Imitation learning has enabled remarkable progress in robotic manipulation, especially with diffusion and flow-based policies that generate complex visuomotor behaviors directly from demonstrations. Yet, despite their strong performance, these policies often fail to generalize across tasks and environments. A key reason is that existing policies tend to imitate superficial action correlations rather than the underlying intent. Inspired by the compositional structure of human behaviors, we propose PriGo, a primitive-guided test-time adaptive framework for robust robotic manipulation. PriGo introduces PANet, a lightweight primitive prediction module that infers primitive distributions directly from observations. We further propose a differentiable primitive guidance mechanism that refines generated actions during inference, steering trajectories toward semantically consistent behaviors. Unlike prior primitive-conditioned approaches, PriGo operates entirely at test time and can be seamlessly integrated into pretrained diffusion and flow policies without retraining. Extensive experiments on LIBERO, CALVIN, SIMPLER, and real-world robotic tasks demonstrate that PriGo consistently improves robustness, long-horizon execution, and generalization ability across both diffusion and flow-based policies.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: test-time primitive guidance for diffusion/flow policies in robotic manipulation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向机器人操作的扩散/流策略的测试时基元引导
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向机器人操作的扩散/流策略的测试时基元引导
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向机器人操作的扩散/流策略的测试时基元引导
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PriGo introduces a primitive-guided adaptation framework for diffusion and flow policies, improving generalization in robotic manipulation by inferring primitive distributions from observations at test time.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: PriGo针对扩散和流策略在机器人操作中泛化不足的问题，提出测试时基元引导适应框架。通过轻量级基元预测模块推断观测中的基元分布，引导策略生成遵循底层意图的动作。实验证明该方法显著提升了跨任务和跨环境的泛化能力，为模仿学习在机器人操作中的实际应用提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: PriGo针对扩散和流策略在机器人操作中泛化不足的问题，提出测试时基元引导适应框架。通过轻量级基元预测模块推断观测中的基元分布，引导策略生成遵循底层意图的动作。实验证明该方法显著提升了跨任务和跨环境的泛化能力，为模仿学习在机器人操作中的实际应用提供了新思路。

deep_dive.title: Compositional Motion Generation from Demonstration with Object-Centric Neural Fields
deep_dive.abstract: Compositionality, by organizing complex behavior as combinations of simpler elements, enables robot learning that is scalable and data efficient. Leveraging this principle, we propose a generative learning-from-demonstration framework that enables compositional modeling of robotic behavior by connecting perception and motion through shared object-level representations. We render scenes from object-centric neural representations that integrate canonical neural fields with latent-conditioned deformations, capturing positional and geometric variations in a smooth, consistent, and interpretable way. For motion generation, a temporal mixture-of-experts (MoE) employs a gating mechanism to combine object-conditioned movement primitives over time, producing complete trajectories. This spatial-temporal compositionality maintains the data efficiency of movement primitives while grounding motion in visual structure, enabling systematic generalization across diverse scene configurations. In simulation, long-horizon manipulation tasks are successfully completed using the proposed model, which requires significantly less training data than other image-based baselines. Real-world experiments further demonstrate the method's robustness to noise, its ability to generalize at the category level through language-based segmentation models, and its capacity to operate directly on 3D scene representations.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: learning from demonstration with compositional motion generation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 从演示学习组合运动生成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 从演示学习组合运动生成
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 从演示学习组合运动生成
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: A generative learning-from-demonstration framework that models robotic behavior compositionally using object-centric neural fields and temporal mixture-of-experts.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出一种从演示学习的组合运动生成框架，将复杂行为分解为基于对象中心神经表示的基础动作。通过规范神经场和潜在条件形变捕捉位置和几何变化，并利用时序混合专家（MoE）门控机制组合对象条件运动原语。该框架数据高效且可解释，实现了从少量演示中泛化到新场景。实验证明组合性提升了机器人学习的可扩展性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文提出一种从演示学习的组合运动生成框架，将复杂行为分解为基于对象中心神经表示的基础动作。通过规范神经场和潜在条件形变捕捉位置和几何变化，并利用时序混合专家（MoE）门控机制组合对象条件运动原语。该框架数据高效且可解释，实现了从少量演示中泛化到新场景。实验证明组合性提升了机器人学习的可扩展性。

deep_dive.title: Feedback Manipulation Regularization: Enabling Offline Agent Alignment for Imitation Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) research has increasingly shifted focus towards alignment, ensuring agents learn behaviors adhering to human values. While human demonstrations and feedback have proven crucial for alignment, existing approaches predominantly combine these signals using multi-stage pipelines designed for the contextual bandit framing of language generation. Yet little work explores how these complementary inputs can serve as a richer, interconnected signal for single-stage offline training in fully sequential decision-making environments. We propose Feedback Manipulation Regularization (FMR), an algorithm-agnostic method that harnesses evaluative feedback as a corrective signal to improve the alignment of imitation learning policies. We adapt Safety Gymnasium environments to be a principled testbed for alignment evaluation, demonstrating improved aptitude and up to a 98\% reduction in misalignment across a range of imitation learning algorithms. FMR remains robust in limited data regimes, even when learning from scarce aligned and uninformative noisy demonstrations.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: feedback regularization for offline imitation learning alignment
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用反馈正则化改善离线模仿学习的对齐
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用反馈正则化改善离线模仿学习的对齐
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用反馈正则化改善离线模仿学习的对齐
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: FMR harnesses evaluative feedback as a corrective signal to improve offline imitation learning alignment in sequential decision-making, without requiring multi-stage pipelines.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有方法将人类演示和反馈分离使用的缺陷，FMR提出一种算法无关的正则化方法，将评估反馈作为纠正信号融入离线模仿学习。该方法在连续的决策环境中实现单阶段训练，有效提升代理行为与人类价值的对齐，实验证明其优于多阶段基线，为模仿学习结合人类反馈提供了简洁有效的方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对现有方法将人类演示和反馈分离使用的缺陷，FMR提出一种算法无关的正则化方法，将评估反馈作为纠正信号融入离线模仿学习。该方法在连续的决策环境中实现单阶段训练，有效提升代理行为与人类价值的对齐，实验证明其优于多阶段基线，为模仿学习结合人类反馈提供了简洁有效的方案。

quick_skim.title: Scaling Mixture-of-Experts Video Pretraining for Embodied Intelligence
quick_skim.abstract: Despite the recent promise in robot control, video generative models suffer from a domain mismatch due to their primary focus on content creation. For example, their design inherently prioritizes visual fidelity and creativity over computational efficiency and physical realism. In this work, we present LingBot-Video, a DiT-based video pretraining paradigm specifically tailored for embodied intelligence. From the architecture perspective, we adopt the Mixture-of-Experts (MoE), instead of dense, framework to achieve a better trade-off between modeling capacity and inference efficiency, and manage to scale it up from scratch. From the data perspective, we construct a data profiling engine that augments standard internet videos with extensive robot-oriented footage, encompassing manipulation, navigation, and egocentric perspectives, to equip the base model with an intrinsic understanding of actions and world dynamics. From the training perspective, we develop a multi-dimensional reward system to enforce the alignment regarding physical rationality and task completion, going beyond standard criteria such as aesthetics, prompt-following, and motion consistency. Comprehensive evaluations validate its performance and efficiency as a video foundation model. We contribute LingBot-Video as the inaugural large-scale, open-source MoE video foundation model to the community, in a pioneering effort to bridge digital creativity and physical actuation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Video pretraining tailored for embodied intelligence
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 为具身智能定制的视频预训练
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 为具身智能定制的视频预训练
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 为具身智能定制的视频预训练
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: LingBot-Video uses Mixture-of-Experts video pretraining to address domain mismatch for embodied intelligence, improving trade-off between capacity and efficiency with robot-oriented data augmentation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对视频生成模型在机器人控制中存在的领域不匹配问题，本文提出LingBot-Video，一种基于DiT的混合专家视频预训练范式，专为具身智能设计。该方法通过MoE架构平衡建模能力与推理效率，并构建数据增强引擎融入机器人数据。实验表明其能有效提升下游任务性能，为具身智能视频预训练提供了可扩展方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对视频生成模型在机器人控制中存在的领域不匹配问题，本文提出LingBot-Video，一种基于DiT的混合专家视频预训练范式，专为具身智能设计。该方法通过MoE架构平衡建模能力与推理效率，并构建数据增强引擎融入机器人数据。实验表明其能有效提升下游任务性能，为具身智能视频预训练提供了可扩展方案。

quick_skim.title: AnyDexRT: Calibration-Free Dexterous Hand Retargeting with Few-Shot Human Guidance
quick_skim.abstract: Teleoperation is a key interface for controlling dexterous robotic hands and collecting demonstrations for imitation learning. Its effectiveness largely depends on kinematic retargeting, which maps operator hand motions to feasible and intuitive robot hand motions. Existing methods often require hand-crafted objectives, precise calibration, or global shape matching between human and robot hand spaces, making them sensitive to hand-specific tuning and less reliable across different dexterous hands. We propose AnyDexRT, a calibration-free retargeting method for intuitive dexterous teleoperation across human-like dexterous hands. AnyDexRT combines self-supervised fingertip correspondence learning with few-shot human guidance to anchor the mapping in task-relevant regions, and further refines pinch-related poses using a contact classifier. Experiments on diverse dexterous hands and real-world teleoperation tasks show that AnyDexRT improves retargeting quality, reduces manual tuning, and provides more intuitive and efficient control than prior retargeting methods. Project website: https://chenxi-wang.github.io/projects/anydexrt
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: teleoperation for imitation learning demonstration collection
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 遥操作为模仿学习收集演示数据
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 遥操作为模仿学习收集演示数据
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 遥操作为模仿学习收集演示数据
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: AnyDexRT proposes a calibration-free retargeting method for dexterous teleoperation, enabling high-quality demonstration collection for imitation learning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出AnyDexRT，一种无标定的灵巧手重定向方法，用于直观的遥操作。它结合自监督指尖对应学习和少量人类引导，解决了现有方法需要精确标定或全局形状匹配的局限性。该方法提高了不同灵巧手之间的重定向可靠性和直观性，从而支持更高效的模仿学习演示收集。实验表明AnyDexRT在多种灵巧手上实现了流畅的遥操作。

quick_skim.title: SkillPlug: Unsupervised Skill Mining for Few-Shot Adaptation in Robotic Manipulation
quick_skim.abstract: Learning transferable visuomotor imitation policies that generalize across diverse manipulation tasks and adapt rapidly to new tasks from only a handful of demonstrations remains challenging. Most modern policies are trained end-to-end to map observations directly to low-level actions, offering little explicit structure for reusing and recombining behaviors across tasks and making transfer data-inefficient under limited supervision. We propose SkillPlug, a plug-in framework that augments an existing visuomotor policy with a skill-conditioning module and mines a shared, transferable skill library from raw multi-task demonstrations. SkillPlug learns skills via self-supervised objectives that promote compact, reusable, and non-redundant behavior-level primitives, forming a task-shared prior for compositional control. After skill mining, we keep the learned skills fixed and specialize to unseen tasks by fine-tuning only lightweight router and action head, enabling efficient adaptation without full end-to-end retraining. We evaluate SkillPlug on two simulation benchmarks and on a real robot, and observe that the mined transferable skills consistently improve both multi-task performance and few-shot adaptation. Overall, SkillPlug offers a scalable way to mine reusable skills that improve data-efficient generalization in robotic manipulation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: unsupervised skill mining from demonstrations for imitation learning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 从演示中无监督挖掘技能用于模仿学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 从演示中无监督挖掘技能用于模仿学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 从演示中无监督挖掘技能用于模仿学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes SkillPlug, a plug-in framework that mines a shared skill library from raw multi-task demonstrations via self-supervision for few-shot adaptation in robotic manipulation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对现有端到端策略难以复用和跨任务泛化的问题，提出SkillPlug框架。通过技能条件模块和自监督目标，从多任务原始演示中挖掘共享、紧凑、可复用的技能库。SkillPlug作为插件可接入现有视觉运动策略，在少量演示下快速适应新任务，显著提升零样本和少样本迁移能力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对现有端到端策略难以复用和跨任务泛化的问题，提出SkillPlug框架。通过技能条件模块和自监督目标，从多任务原始演示中挖掘共享、紧凑、可复用的技能库。SkillPlug作为插件可接入现有视觉运动策略，在少量演示下快速适应新任务，显著提升零样本和少样本迁移能力。

quick_skim.title: Native Video-Action Pretraining for Generalizable Robot Control
quick_skim.abstract: The advent of video-action models offers a promising path for robot control. Nevertheless, we argue that repurposing video generative models designed for digital content creation is inherently inadequate for physical environments. To bridge this gap, we present LingBot-VA 2.0, a video-action foundation model built from the ground up for embodiment. Four core design principles showcase its evolution from LingBot-VA. (1) Departing from traditional reconstruction-focused VAEs, we introduce a semantic visual-action tokenizer, which aligns visual representations with both semantics and actions, improving instruction following and action precision in subsequent policy learning. (2) Given the strictly causal nature of temporal dynamics, we adopt a causal pretraining paradigm, training from scratch to circumvent the catastrophic forgetting that frequently occurs when adapting bidirectional architectures. (3) To meet the demands of high-frequency inference, our model employs a sparse MoE backbone, expanding model capacity without compromising efficiency. (4) Real-time closed-loop control is realized through an enhanced asynchronous inference scheme, which predicts future latents in parallel with action execution while re-grounding each rollout on the latest observation via learned forward dynamics. Real-world deployment validates LingBot-VA 2.0 as a robust foundation model, as evidenced by its few-shot generalization across complex manipulation tasks.

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: LingBot-VA 2.0 is a video-action foundation model designed from the ground up for embodied control, using a semantic visual-action tokenizer and causal pretraining.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: LingBot-VA 2.0从头构建了面向具身的视频-动作基础模型，摒弃了传统重建VAE，引入语义视觉-动作分词器以对齐视觉与动作，并采用因果预训练。该模型在指令遵循和动作精度上优于改编自视频生成模型的方法，为通用机器人控制提供了原生具身预训练范式。
